title: ivcbench: An Immune-Aware Benchmark of Perturbation-Prediction Generalization
description:
Code, deposited result tables, and figure scripts for the Briefings in Bioinformatics Problem Solving Protocol "Toward Immune Virtual Cells: An Immune-Aware Benchmark of Perturbation-Prediction Generalization" (Lee & Ryu).

The benchmark evaluates whether single-cell perturbation-prediction models (foundation, latent/compositional, prior-graph, optimal-transport, and hybrid families) generalize across the axes that matter for immunology — cell-context transfer, unseen perturbations, unseen donors, and readout modality — and whether they beat a set of pre-registered simple baselines (the universal floor). The contribution is evaluation methodology, not a new model. This archive contains the GPU-free benchmark core (leak-proof split builder and leak auditor, applicability registry, the three immune-aware metrics, cluster-bootstrap statistics, and the runner), the figure and analysis scripts, the dataset download scripts, and the deposited paper-level result tables that reproduce every figure and supplementary table without re-running any model.

creators.name: Lee, Chanhee
